intro: Glandulifolia biseriata, commonly known as Glandularia, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Verbenaceae family, native to South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Glandulifolia biseriata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various herbal remedies, such as extracts and tinctures, to promote health and well-being.
